person.name: Cristher Castro

She co-created, along with her team, the first Kubernetes Community Days in Colombia. This event paved the way for the expansion of Cloud Native Groups throughout the region. Cristher's foresight and operational expertise have been fundamental to the success of both Cuemby and the Cloud Native Latam Group. Recognizing the region's need for in-depth knowledge of cloud-native applications, Cristher championed the LFS250 (Linux Foundation Course) and administered the KCNA certifications donated by CNCF to the region. These two initiatives heightened awareness of cloud-native technologies and certifications, fortifying local proficiency in open-source solutions. She continues to promote and advocate for the recognition of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) in the region, addressing both business and community aspects. Cristher's leadership and operational prowess exemplify her unwavering commitment to fostering a thriving Latam tech community. https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-latin-america/ https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-medellin/ https://www.cncf.io/reports/kcd-colombia-2023/ https://www.cuemby.com/about-us https://www.linkedin.com/company/fundaci%C3%B3n-hispana-de-cloud-native/?viewAsMember=true https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-colombia/ https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombia/?viewAsMember=truehttps://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombiaWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Becoming a CNCF ambassador empowers me to further my dedication to community growth in the LATAM region. I deeply believe that the region's success hinges on the symbiotic collaboration of professionals, students, and companies. It's this harmony that forms the core of my mission: to cultivate a thriving community ecosystem centered around open-source and cloud-native technologies. Through this role, I am positioned to amplify awareness about these technologies and the transformative opportunities they introduce. My operational and logistics expertise is set to facilitate more meetups and events, thus kindling the formation of new communities. Collaborating with fellow leaders and ambassadors provides a unique platform to unite our efforts, ensuring we collectively advance our shared vision.

I am a DevOps Engineer specializing in delivering cutting-edge client solutions using Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), GitOps, AI, RAG, and cloud-native solutions. Working with a professional team, I have a proven track record of implementing and maintaining numerous AKS clusters, automating complex deployment and configuration pipelines, and enforcing rigorous security and compliance standards.

person.name: Min Lawi Aung Win
person.bio: I am a tech professional from Myanmar with a background in networking, and I started learning cloud technologies in 2024. I am interested in cloud-native tools, especially Kubernetes and related CNCF projects, and enjoy learning through community events and meetups. I like sharing and discussing ideas about modern infrastructure with others. In my free time, I enjoy playing the guitar and jamming with friends.

person.name: Sachin Kumar
person.bio: I am a senior Cloud Engineer at Ellucian with keen interest in Kubernetes technology .

person.name: Tristan Smagghe
person.bio: I'm a software engineer at Infomaniak and mostly work on Kubernetes. I also present talks from time to time and the first one I presented was at Silicon Chalet in Geneva. You can find out more about me at https://yewolf.fr

person.name: Ivan Voloboyev
person.bio: I am a DevOps, SysOps, and Platform Engineer with a strong background in network engineering, focused on cloud platforms, automation, and building scalable, reliable infrastructure. My expertise spans Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, ArgoCD, and modern GitOps practices across AWS and GCP. I actively participate in the cloud-native community and enjoy sharing knowledge through meetups and hands-on collaboration. As a perpetual learner, I’m always exploring new tools and approaches that improve the developer and operational experience.

person.name: Viktor Farcic
person.bio: Viktor Farcic is a lead rapscallion at Upbound and a published author. He is a host of the YouTube channel DevOps Toolkit and a co-host of DevOps Paradox.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Viktor Farcic is excited to join the CNCF Ambassadors program because he believes in the power of open source software to transform the world. He is passionate about helping others learn about and adopt CNCF technologies, and he is eager to contribute to the CNCF community in new and innovative ways. Viktor is also excited to be part of a team of dedicated and talented individuals who are working to make the world a better place through open source software.

person.name: Arun Kumar Anike
person.bio: I am an Senior SRE professional with extensive experience in Kubernetes and OpenShift. My expertise lies in CNCF technologies, cloud-native automation, and infrastructure reliability. I actively engage with the cloud-native community, sharing insights through meetups and discussions, and I am passionate about driving best practices in DevOps and container security

person.name: Frederico Muñoz
person.bio: Frederico started working with GNU and Linux in the mid-90s out of sheer curiosity, a decision that strongly influenced his life. Actively involved in the free software and open source community from early on, he made a path from programming and system administration towards data science, cloud computing and enterprise architecture. In recent years he has been actively involved in projects touching such diverse areas as Quantum computing, IoT, and analytics, and actively participating in the Kubernetes project as part of the Release Team, SIG Contribex, and others. He currently leads the Cloud and Architecture domain at SAS, for the South West & East Europe region. He holds a BA in Archaeology from the University of Lisbon, where he is an MA student and where he also took Physics.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I see being a CNCF Ambassador as a great opportunity to engage with others both in the technical aspects of cloud-native computing and in the many ways that those interested can become active members of the community. Cloud-native technology has largely been developed through open collaboration, and being an Ambassador is taking a step towards more visible participation in building the community - and learning from it.

person.name: Hari Krishna Sigicherla
person.bio: I’m a Cloud Devops engineer with 12years of IT experiences. I specialized in cloud platforms(AWS/AZURE), provisioning infrastructure to managing cloud-native application deployments. I've good exposure to Cloud, CI/CD, Security & Observability working on various roles & responsibilities. Certified in AWS, AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform & Databricks. Excited to explore the new opportunities as a CNCF ambassador.

person.name: Julius Wolf
person.bio: DevOps Engineer based in Germany. With passion for Cloud, Kubernetes und cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Ferhat Sanuç
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer at Rabobank with over 12 years of professional experience. I began my career as a Linux System Administrator and have since specialized in Kubernetes, OpenShift, and related cloud-native technologies

person.name: Jérémy Albrecht
person.bio: I'm Jérémy, a former backend developer who has transitioned into the dynamic world of DevOps Engineering. I weave together Azure Cloud, Kubernetes, Docker, Infrastructure as Code, Ansible and automation to create seamless, efficient solutions.

person.name: Andrius Cibulskis
person.bio: I am Platform/DevOps/SRE Engineer. Passionate about Kubernetes and all the CNCF technologies since 2017. My journey with technology began in the early days of computing, back when the 286 processor was cutting-edge. Exploring FreeBSD, Linux and the early days of the internet sparked my passion for technology, problem-solving, and innovation.

person.name: Maurizio Venturelli
person.bio: As a dedicated Cloud Solution Architect with a strong focus on Kubernetes management, I bring specialized expertise in container orchestration and cloud-native solutions.  My passion for designing scalable and efficient cloud architectures has enabled me to deliver robust solutions that meet diverse business needs.  Recent projects I have worked on involved the implementation of a Kubernetes-as-a-Service solution to simplify cluster provisioning and lifecycle management, serving the needs of both internal development teams and external clients.

person.name: Vishnu Prasad V V
person.bio: I am a seasoned DevOps Engineer with 8 years of experience specializing in multi-cloud environments currently working at Ernst & Young. My expertise lies in designing, implementing, and optimizing DevOps practices across various cloud platforms, including AWS, Azure, and GCP. I thrive in fast-paced, agile environments and have a proven track record of delivering robust solutions that enhance software development and deployment processes.

person.name: Jonas Thaarup Løvig
person.bio: Jonas has been working with cloud-native technologies since 2019, specializing in data infrastructure on Kubernetes, mainly Apache Kafka via Strimzi and Elasticsearch/OpenSearch. These days he's channeling that experience into building a machine learning developer platform. He's a firm believer that getting things running is the easy part and it's designing for failure that actually matters. Jonas has a thing for making reliability and resilience click for everyone in the room, from engineers to leadership, because when infrastructure fails or external forces strike and the dashboards stay green, that's when resilience speaks for itself. That mindset, combined with years of hands-on cloud-native work, earned him the Kubestronaut distinction.

person.name: Samet Ustaoglu
person.bio: I am a Cloud Architect and DevOps Engineer with expertise in Kubernetes, GitOps, and cloud infrastructure automation. I enjoy optimizing systems, building scalable solutions, and sharing my knowledge through my medium account https://medium.com/@sametustaoglu

person.name: Ahmed Okila
person.bio: I am a DevSecOps Engineer with a strong focus on cloud-native security and automation. I have spent significant time studying and working with CNCF technologies, including tools within the Kubernetes ecosystem, to build secure, scalable, and reliable infrastructure. My interests include DevSecOps practices, container security, CI/CD pipeline hardening, and infrastructure as code. I am passionate about continuously learning and applying cloud-native technologies to improve software delivery and security practices.

person.name: Bin Bin Yang
person.bio: My name is Bin Bin Yang. I am a motivated and driven CloudNative DevOps engineer focused on automation and Infrastructure as Code, designing scalable and repeatable cloud environments. With strong experience deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters—including bare-metal setups

person.name: Claudiu Avatamanitei
person.bio: I am an experienced IT professional with a strong background in design and implementation of complex software platforms, reliability and observability engineering, and defense in depth security. I am currently focused on enabling digital and cloud-native transformations at scale in the mortgage servicing business, by providing harmonious foundational technology ecosystems composed of vendor neutral, CNCF managed open source de facto standards. With extensive hands on expertise in Kubernetes (EKS, OCP), Istio, GitOps and ArgoCD, OTEL and Kiali, IaC (Terraform, Crossplane, Spacelift, Puppet), software development (Python, Java, C++), and several CI/CD platforms, I enable teams to focus on business value delivery and ensure low stress, successful development and operations of comprehensive software platforms.

person.name: Deepak Gupta
person.bio: I am Deepak Gupta, a seasoned Cloud Migration Expert specializing in AWS and Azure cloud platforms. I have extensive experience in migrating applications from on-premises to the cloud, with a particular focus on containerization using Kubernetes. My expertise lies in transforming traditional applications into scalable and efficient cloud-native architectures. My work is instrumental in driving digital transformation and optimizing performance in the new cloud environment.

person.name: Kannan N
person.bio: I’m Kannan, an experienced software engineer and cloud-native enthusiast with over a decade of innovation in the tech industry. My expertise as a DevOps engineer and cloud architect allows me to design and manage scalable, resilient, and efficient digital ecosystems. With a deep mastery of Kubernetes, I’ve helped organizations streamline operations and optimize cloud-native infrastructure. My passion for automation and cloud technologies drives me to constantly improve system performance and ensure high availability.

person.name: Michele Paci
person.bio: Hi, I’m Michele Paci and I work as a Cloud Architect and DevOps Engineer. Recently, I’ve been focusing on effectively embedding DevOps in organizations, and I’m interested in infrastructure reliability and application design in cloud environments. I’ve also been managing migrations to Kubernetes, being a big fan of monitoring and automation tools.

person.name: Baikuntha Bana Bihari Sahoo

I have a strong focus on Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystem technologies, with interests spanning container orchestration, observability, GitOps, and building scalable, resilient platforms. Deeply passionate about DevOps and SRE practices, I continuously enhance my knowledge of modern cloud-native ecosystems. I actively engage with the cloud-native community through knowledge sharing, technical discussions, and community events, and regularly write and speak about cloud-native best practices. Always eager to grow and learn, I am currently exploring AI and ML to integrate these emerging capabilities into future projects.

person.name: Elmer Manquiria
person.bio: I’m an IT professional with a strong passion for cloud-native technologies, networking, and security. I actively keep up with new tech trends and enjoy building secure, efficient, and reliable systems while learning something new every day.

person.name: Geoffrey Shelton Okot
person.bio: I am a seasoned cloud-data architect and big data evangelist with over 15 years of experience driving large-scale digital transformation across enterprise environments.  As a certified Kubestronaut I specialize in architecting decoupled Kubernetes solutions and container-native architectures that transform complex data challenges into scalable, future-ready platforms. Recognized as one of the world's top 10 Cloudera community contributors,I've has consistently delivered mission-critical cloud migrations and big data implementations for global organizations.  My expertise spans from Kubernetes orchestration to DevSecOps practices, enabling organizations to achieve operational agility while maintaining enterprise-grade security and compliance.

person.name: Marvin Mottet
person.bio: I’m a naturally curious problem-solver who loves diving into new challenges ! My work in IT has led me to develop skills and a strong interest in fullstack development and DevOps. As a Kubestronaut, I’m excited to keep learning, sharing, and contributing to cloud-native technologies.
